description: Number serialization: 11 representative values across the ECMAScript Number::toString branch matrix referenced by RFC 8785 §3.2.2.3. Covers zero, small signed integers, max safe integer (2^53 − 1), integer-valued float (1.0 → '1'), simple and signed fractions, and the four exponent-branch boundary cases (1e-7 → '1e-7' scientific; 1e-6 → '0.000001' decimal; 1e20 → integer form with 20 zeros; 1e21 → '1e+21' scientific). Catches Python-style trailing '.0' on integer-valued floats, wrong-side-of-boundary scientific/decimal emission, and uppercase 'E' in exponents. IEEE 754 shortest-round-trip edge cases (e.g., 0.1 + 0.2) are deferred to a future vector explicitly seeded from RFC 8785 appendix or a second implementation.
source: hand-verified against docs/canonicalization.md §7.2, §7.5, §7.6, §7.9, RFC 8785 §3.2.2.3, RFC 8785 §3.2.3, and the ECMAScript Number::toString algorithm by Fabio M. S., 2026-04-20
